rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARILYN ANN MALLEN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Orders: 1) To Recover Some Of The Funds Distributed From Trust; 2) Compelling Co-Trustees To Account And Report; 3) Construing Certain Provisions Of Trust Instrument; 4) Instructing Trustees To Deposit Trust Funds In A Blocked Account, Until Such Time As The Expenses Associated With Administration Of Decedent?S Estate Are Determined
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petition requests various forms of relief against the trustees concerning the internal affairs of the trust under Probate Code section 17200(b)(1) (construction of trust instrument), (b)(6)(instructing trustees), and (b)(7)(B)(C)(trust information and accounting). Only a trustee or a beneficiary can petition for relief concerning the internal affairs of the trust (see section 17200(a)). Daniel Mallen, one of the trust beneficiaries, executed a "notice of assignment lien" purporting to assign his distributive share of the trust to the petitioner (who was representing Daniel Mallen at the time of the assignment) to secure payment of legal fees and costs Daniel Mallen individually owed to the petitioner. Article 6.2 of the governing trust provides, however, that: "The interests of the beneficiaries under this instrument are not transferrable by voluntary or involuntary assignment or by operation of law, and shall be free from the claims of creditors and from attachment, execution, bankruptcy, and other legal process, to the maximum extent permitted by law." Probate Code section 15301(a) provides in relevant part that: "If the trust instrument provides that a beneficiary's interest in principal is not subject to voluntary or involuntary transfer, the beneficiary's interest in principal may not be transferred and is not subject to enforcement of a money judgment until paid to the beneficiary." While section 15301(b) allows a judgment creditor of a trust beneficiary to petition under CCP section 709.010 for an order directing a trustee to satisfy the money judgment out of principal that became due and payable to the judgment debtor beneficiary, the petitioner has not obtained a money judgment against Daniel Mallen necessary to invoke CCP section 709.010 and compel the trustees to pay the petitioner's fees and costs directly from Daniel Mallen's undistributed share of the trust, not to exceed 25% thereof (see Probate Code section 15306.5(b)). 2) The petition also requests relief under Probate Code section 850 against the trustees to recover sufficient funds from the beneficiaries who received partial distribution from the trust, for the purpose of preserving such funds for payment of expenses of administration of the settlor's probate estate, including the petitioner's fees for ordinary and extraordinary legal services to the estate, that may or may not be approved by the Court in the related PES case. Section 850(a)(3)(C) allows any interested person to petition "where the property of the trust is claimed to be subject to a creditor of the settlor of the trust". "Any interested person" presumably includes a creditor of the settlor of the trust, but the petitioner is a creditor of a trust beneficiary (who is not the settlor). See also Arluk Medical Center Industrial Group, Inc. v. Dobler (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 1324 ("Nothing in our opinion was intended to suggest a duty exists on the part of the trustee to withhold any otherwise authorized distribution of property to ensure the availability of trust assets for the ultimate benefit of a third party with only a disputed claim. --- The general rule in both statute and common law is that the trustee has no obligation to third party claimants to preserve trust assets.") PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF WILLIAM ESTRADA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE CONSERVATORSHIP of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/6/24. (No earlier availability and no more hearing spots on 1/30/24.) (1) The original 13100 affidavit does not appear to have the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-300 required for decedent's dying on or after 4/1/22. (2) The conservator is still accounting for each bank account separately but the subtotals of receipts appear to total $700,806.35, not $511,958.02. Transfers between accounts are not receipts and should not be reported. [Probate Code Section 1063(b).] (3) The Summary of Account for the account following the date of death shows non-cash assets totaling $116,405.72, but the schedule of assets on hand as of 2/3/23 shows the total CV as $116,373.58. (The FMV values also do not agree.) (4) The verification of this third amended petition refers to the second amended petition. (For some reason, Schedule E (assets on hand) at the end of the account, was collated to precede all of the detailed schedules supporting the summary of account, further confusing review. Also, additional I&As were filed on 12/22/23 that have odd captions (e.g., Corrected Partial No. 2 of 2, when there was no Partial No. 1) and do not appear to have been necessary, as there is no need to appraise assets on the date of a conservatee's death. No I&A should have item 4 answered when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed.) Unless a fourth amended accounting accounts for all cash together, not separated out for each bank account, please do not amend the accounting again. Deadline is 2/26/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

The missing information includes: i) Whether any heir or beneficiary is currently or has previously been confined in any facility described in Probate Code section 9202(b) and whether notice under that subdivision is required; and ii) Whether there are any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution. 2) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 3) Has the petitioner recorded a certified copy of the Court's 8/2/23 order, as required by the settlement terms? 4) The decedent died more than 5 years ago, but no interim accounts have been filed. i) Does the petitioner anticipate obtaining waivers of account from all other beneficiaries? ii) Does the petitioner intend to apply for permission to enlarge a final account period under LRSF 14.34(A) if the account is not waived?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTORIA C. DRAYTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Entitlement To Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/19/2024, as the following issues (see tentative ruling for 11/07/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) Please confirm whether there was any mortgage on the estate real property at the time of the predeceased spouse's death. 4) Has the estate real property been sold? If so, please state the amount of cash currently held by the estate. 5) All the persons having legal custody of and residing with Ean Foster (minor) must be identified and receive a separate copy of notice. Direct notice must also be given to the minor. CRC 7.51(d). 6) Patrice Green's address listed on the proof of service is slightly different from the address set forth on Exhibit A attached to the petition. 7) A proof must be filed to establish a parent-child relationship as to each unidentified child of Henry Lucas and Peter Drayton. 8) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) must be filed to show efforts made to locate each unidentified child of Henry Lucas and Peter Drayton. Whether notice can be prescribed in any manner other than mailing (telephone, e-mail, social media, etc.) must also be addressed. CRC 7.52(c). 9) Please explain why no share is allocated to each unidentified child of Peter Drayton. Note: Mecca Thompson's objection on file states that Alice Lucas died without issue and disputes the proposed distribution of 1/280 (= 1/2 x 1/10 x 1/14) to Sabrina Williams and Dominic Williams as the purported surviving issue of Alice Lucas. The objection also states that Henry Lucas left no surviving issue and disputes the proposed distribution of 1/220 (= 1/2 x 1/10 x 1/11) to Henry Lucas' unidentified child.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/5/24 at Counsel's request. A proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition, any amendment or supplement thereto, and proof(s) of service must be received by the assigned examiner by 2/23/24. The face of the Notice of Hearing must indicate the title of the pleading to be heard, including any amendment to the pleading. The proof of service on the reverse side of the Notice of Hearing should show the date of mailing of the Notice. Notice must include the procedures for making a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (Based on the relief in this petition, additional time to administer the estate would be granted for some time in May 2024. Unless more time is requested in an amendment, no more than six months from the date the petition was signed will be granted.)

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/05/2024 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 01/19/2024). The following issues must be addressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-115 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 2) The legal description of the subject real property interest must be included in the proposed order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE DORIS J. MIDDLETON AND GEORGE MIDDLETON REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Under Probate Code 850 For Order Determining Payments Re Fire Damage On Trust Real Property Is Property Of The Doris J. Middleton And George
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/6/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been submitted or lodged. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service has been filed. If the notice did not include the language regarding remote appearance procedures, notice of the 3/6/24 hearing must be served with such instructions attached. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) Exhibit D to the petition is the copy of George Middleton's will, not the documentation reported to have been received from the Fire Victim Trust. (Why can't the funds be received by the petitioner and then deposited into an account held by the trust? Would that trigger a tax issue?) A supplement must be filed, signed and verified by the petitioner, submitting the missing exhibit. A copy of the supplement must be served. [CRC 7.53(b). Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/26/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TANYA SAMUEL DZHIBRAILOVA LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Instructing Trustee To Place Property In Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/19/2024. Notes emailed to counsel on 01/26/2024.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WINIFRED OCTAVIA HUEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
